title: Simulation test assertions and CI integration
description: Add assertions to the gameplay scenario: at least 1 device repaired, hospital balance non-zero, no Lua errors thrown, world state is valid (no nil entities, no corrupted room list). Output pass/fail to stdout so CI can detect failures. Wire up a PowerShell/shell script (scripts/run_simulation_test.ps1) that sets SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy, launches CorsixTH with --headless-test, and exits non-zero on failure. Depends on: gameplay scenario script bead.

title: Headless test runner: SDL dummy driver launch
description: Set SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy before SDL.init so the game can launch without a real display. Add a --headless-test CLI flag to CorsixTH.lua/app.lua. When the flag is set: skip all rendering (no drawFrame calls), suppress UI construction, and load a named test script after level init instead of entering normal gameplay. This is the prerequisite for all automated simulation tests.

title: Infrastructure failure: wire adviser messages and IT-theme remaining earthquake strings
Fix headless SDL: SDL_WINDOW_OPENGL prevents dummy/offscreen driver
description: The C++ renderer (th_gfx_sdl.cpp) passes SDL_WINDOW_OPENGL to SDL_CreateWindow unconditionally. This causes TH.surface() to throw when SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy (no OpenGL support) or SDL_VIDEODRIVER=offscreen (not compiled in this SDL build). Fix: detect headless mode in C++ and skip the OPENGL flag (or use a software/null renderer). Until fixed, integration tests require a real or virtual display (Xvfb on Linux CI).

title: Terminal Triage total conversion
description: Deliver the IT-repair total conversion described in docs\\it-total-conversion-mod-gdd.md and scoped in docs\\it-total-conversion-technical-plan.md. This epic groups the implementation work needed to ship a standalone Terminal Triage experience on top of CorsixTH.
acceptance_criteria: A coherent set of child epics exists covering bootstrap, re-theme, content remap, presentation, and deeper system work for the Terminal Triage mod.

title: Bootstrap alternate Lua-root mod package
description: Create the standalone mod package layout around CorsixTH's --lua-dir bootstrap hook. Establish a mirrored Lua, Levels, Campaigns, and Graphics tree, add launcher/config support, and prove the mod can boot from its own root without modifying a user's base install in place.
acceptance_criteria: The mod can be launched from its own content root via --lua-dir, its config is isolated, and custom levels/campaigns/graphics resolve from the mod package.

title: Define Terminal Triage package layout
description: Specify the standalone Terminal Triage folder structure around the alternate Lua-root approach, including Lua, Levels, Campaigns, Graphics, config, and launcher placement.
acceptance_criteria: A concrete package layout is documented and agreed, with each top-level folder and bootstrap file accounted for.

title: Create mod launcher strategy
description: Define and implement the launcher approach for starting CorsixTH with the Terminal Triage content root, including the expected --lua-dir and config-file arguments.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear launcher strategy that starts the mod via --lua-dir and targets the mod-specific config path.

title: Create isolated mod config baseline
description: Prepare a mod-local configuration baseline so Terminal Triage can run without reusing or overwriting a player's main CorsixTH configuration and hotkeys files.
acceptance_criteria: The mod has its own config and hotkeys baseline, and the expected isolation from the user's main config is documented.

title: Stand up minimal alternate Lua root
description: Create the smallest mirrored content tree needed to prove the alternate Lua-root bootstrap works, including the minimum Lua-side structure required by the mod package.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal alternate Lua-root tree exists and is sufficient for startup experiments.

title: Verify path resolution from alternate root
description: Confirm that CorsixTH resolves Lua, Levels, Campaigns, and Graphics relative to the Terminal Triage content root when launched via --lua-dir.
acceptance_criteria: Path resolution is validated for the alternate root, with no accidental dependency on the base in-repo content tree for the tested surfaces.

title: Prove custom campaign and level discovery
description: Add a minimal custom campaign and level pair to the Terminal Triage package and verify they are visible and loadable through the existing custom campaign and custom level flows.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal campaign/level pair is discovered from the mod package and can be loaded successfully.

title: Prove graphics override path
description: Validate that the mod package can use Graphics\\file_mapping.txt and related graphics settings from its own content root.
acceptance_criteria: Graphics override behavior is confirmed from the mod package, or the exact gap/blocker is documented.

title: Document bootstrap workflow for developers
description: Capture the recommended development workflow for iterating in-repo first and then extracting the work into the standalone Terminal Triage package.
acceptance_criteria: Developers have a concise workflow document covering package structure, launch method, and the transition from in-repo iteration to standalone packaging.

title: Create bootstrap smoke-test checklist
description: Define the bootstrap acceptance checklist covering startup, isolated config usage, alternate-root path resolution, custom campaign loading, and graphics override validation.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable smoke-test checklist exists for validating the bootstrap/package milestone.

title: Build text-first IT total conversion layer
description: Re-theme the game in player-facing text before deep behavior changes. Add a dedicated Terminal Triage language layer, replace hospital terminology, update adviser and tooltip copy, and rewrite campaign and level framing so the game reads like an IT repair business sim while preserving internal IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Core UI, room names, staff labels, disease names, adviser text, and campaign briefings present a consistent IT-repair fantasy with minimal hospital terminology leakage.

title: Write IT-theme English string overrides
Write language layer tests for Terminal Triage
Remap gameplay content and campaign to IT repair
description: Translate the playable content skeleton into Terminal Triage. Remap disease definitions to device issues, room definitions to service departments, objects and machines to repair equipment, and build a dedicated campaign and level set tuned to the IT repair progression laid out in the GDD.
acceptance_criteria: A playable Terminal Triage campaign exists with remapped issues, rooms, objects, pricing, research, and level progression, while preserving stable internal IDs for the first release.

title: Create Level 2: Neighborhood Repair Shop
Create Level 3: Student Tech Hub
Write level content validation tests
Replace presentation with IT-themed art and audio
description: Remove the remaining hospital presentation by delivering custom graphics mappings, sprites, icons, menu art, and IT-themed music or subtitle-aligned audio presentation. This epic should make the mod look and sound like its own game rather than a renamed hospital sim.
acceptance_criteria: Custom graphics mappings are in place, major hospital-facing visuals are replaced or overridden, and the mod's presentation is thematically coherent even if custom spoken audio remains partially deferred.

title: Sprite sheet override not yet supported in graphics pipeline
description: CorsixTH's Graphics:loadSpriteTable() calls app:readDataFile() which always reads from the original Theme Hospital install directory. There is no hook to redirect sprite sheet loading to TerminalTriage/Graphics/. Only animation .dat files (via file_mapping.txt) are currently overridable. To support IT-themed sprite replacements in Phase 4, the engine's loadSpriteTable must be extended to check a custom_graphics_folder before falling back to readDataFile.

title: Patch remaining hospital string leaks in IT language file
description: Add IT-themed overrides for strings that still show hospital framing during gameplay: room_descriptions.* (inflation clinic, staff room, etc.), level_progress.cured_enough_patients + hospital_value_enough, tooltip.casebook.cure_requirement.* + cure_type.*, tooltip.objects.reception_desk + litter, adviser.goals.lose.kill. These are the primary visible hospital leaks during normal campaign play. Add TDD tests to verify all new strings are IT-themed and contain no hospital/medical terminology.

title: Add IT-specific systems and behavioral extensions
description: Implement the deeper mechanics that are unique to the IT setting after the MVP content conversion is stable. This includes malware-style outbreak behavior, infrastructure-failure events, QA or burn-in workflows, contract or SLA concepts if still valuable, and revised customer failure outcomes where the existing medical framing becomes too visible.
acceptance_criteria: Post-MVP systems unique to Terminal Triage are implemented selectively without destabilizing the core management loop, and high-risk behavior changes are validated against the technical plan.

title: Remove grim reaper: device-loss outcome for IT mod
description: When a device cannot be repaired (patient 'dies'), the hospital-specific grim reaper + lava hole sequence plays. For Terminal Triage this must be suppressed. Override humanoid_actions/die.lua in the mod to skip both the grim reaper and the heaven-death sequences; after the fall animation the entity is simply despawned (device written off). Also fix App.loadLuaFolder in the mod to merge base + mod directories so that only the overridden file needs to live in the mod's humanoid_actions/ folder.

title: Align disease contagious flags with IT fiction
description: Review and fix disease.contagious flags in TerminalTriage/Lua/diseases/ so that only network/malware diseases can trigger epidemic events. Hardware defects and physical damage (Battery Balloon, Port Collapse, Printer Diplomacy Failure, etc.) should be non-contagious. Software/malware diseases (Ransomwear, Privacy Spill, Phishing Hangover, Latency Gremlins, etc.) should remain or become contagious. This makes the IT epidemic system semantically coherent - only malware actually spreads through the facility network. Add TDD tests to verify each disease contagious flag.
close_reason: 15 disease files updated with IT-aligned contagious flags; transparency (Privacy Spill) set contagious=true; 14 hardware/user-error diseases set contagious=false; 34 new TDD tests pass (175 total)

title: Add automated gameplay smoke test
description: Parent bead for automated gameplay simulation test suite. Covers headless launch, tick acceleration, scripted scenario (rooms/staff/patients), and assertions. Children: headless runner, tick accelerator, scenario script, assertions.

title: Override PA subtitle announcements with IT-themed text
Gameplay scenario script: rooms, staff, and customer flow
description: Write a Lua scenario script (TerminalTriage/Lua/tests/simulation_scenario.lua) that exercises a complete game flow: (1) load Level 1, (2) place reception desk + Triage Desk room, (3) hire a Service Desk Coordinator and a Technician, (4) wait for the first customer to arrive and complete a repair, (5) place a second room and hire more staff, (6) advance several in-game months. Uses TheApp:runSimulatedTicks() from the tick accelerator. Depends on: tick accelerator bead.

title: Tick accelerator: drive world:onTick in tight loop for tests
description: In test mode (--headless-test), replace the SDL timer-driven tick with a tight Lua loop: call world:onTick() N times per real-time frame with no sleep, effectively running simulation at thousands of game-hours per second. Add TheApp:runSimulatedTicks(n) helper. Also expose a world:advanceToDate(year, month, day) convenience that ticks until that date is reached. Depends on: headless runner bead.
